3n-4d-aswan-luxor
Aswan → Luxor
A short, refined sailing experience for travellers with limited time — the temples and ancient quarries of the Nile between Aswan and Luxor, unhurried, in three nights.

Timeless charm meets the gentle rhythm of the river. This express voyage carries you north from Aswan to Luxor, visiting the distinctive double temple of Kom Ombo, the great sandstone quarries of Gebel el-Silsila, the superbly preserved Temple of Horus at Edfu, and the ancient city of El Kab — with traditional sailing and long, relaxed hours on deck.
Classic Cruise
aswan-luxor-classic
Veda I
Veda II
Veda III
Veda IV
Kom Ombo
Gebel el-Silsila
Edfu Temple
El Kab
Esna Lock
/images/dsc-1797-pano-1280x800.jpg
/images/aswan-temple-1280x800.jpg

Three nights aboard your dahabiya, sailing north with the current from Aswan to Luxor. Highlights include Kom Ombo, the sandstone quarries of Gebel el-Silsila, the Temple of Horus at Edfu, and the ancient city of El Kab, with a passage through the Esna Lock before arriving in Luxor — the ancient city of Thebes.
Pace
Express · relaxed
Dining
Local & international
Aswan
Luxor
Embarkation in Aswan — Kom Ombo & Gebel el-Silsila

Your journey begins in Aswan, where you board the vessel around midday before sailing north along the Nile. Enjoy lunch and your first views of traditional Nile villages and desert landscapes, then visit the remarkable Temple of Kom Ombo before continuing to the dramatic sandstone cliffs of Gebel el-Silsila.
/images/egypte-veda-248-van-577-2-1280x800.jpg
Begin sailing toward Kom Ombo (approx. 4 hours downstream with the Nile current). Enjoy lunch and your first views of traditional Nile villages and desert landscapes.

Visit the remarkable Temple of Kom Ombo, uniquely dedicated to two gods: Sobek, the crocodile god, and Horus the Elder. The temple's symmetrical design and beautifully carved reliefs make it one of the most distinctive temples in Egypt.
Continue sailing north to Gebel el-Silsila (approximately 3 hours).
Overnight
Moored at Gebel el-Silsila, surrounded by the dramatic sandstone cliffs that once supplied stone for many of Egypt's great temples.
Gebel el-Silsila — Edfu & El Kab

This morning explores one of the Nile Valley's lesser-visited but historically important quarry sites, before sailing on to the best-preserved temple in Egypt at Edfu and continuing toward the ancient city of El Kab.
/images/Gebel_El.jpg
Visit Gebel el-Silsila, the principal sandstone quarry of ancient Egypt. Explore the rock-cut shrines, stelae, and quarries where blocks were extracted for temples across the country.
Sail north toward Edfu (approximately 3 hours).

Visit the magnificent Temple of Horus at Edfu, the best-preserved temple in Egypt. Built during the Ptolemaic period, its towering pylons, monumental statues of Horus, and detailed reliefs illustrate ancient rituals and mythological scenes.
Resume sailing toward El Kab (about 4 hours).
El Kab, near the ancient city of Nekheb, once an important religious centre dedicated to the vulture goddess Nekhbet.
Esna & Sailing to Luxor
An early start allows for smooth navigation through the Nile's only lock on this section of the river, followed by a full day's sail through the fertile Nile Valley to Luxor.
/images/Esna_lock.jpg
Early morning sailing toward Esna (approximately 2 hours).
Passage through the Esna Lock, an interesting experience as the vessel is raised to match the water level upstream.
Continue sailing north toward Luxor (approximately 6 hours), enjoying the scenery of the Nile Valley with its fertile fields, palm groves, and riverside villages.
Moored in Luxor.
Disembarkation in Luxor
After breakfast, disembark in Luxor — the ancient city of Thebes and one of the world's greatest archaeological regions.
/images/karnak-1280x800.jpg
Morning
After breakfast on board, disembark in Luxor, marking the end of your Nile journey.
Is this itinerary too short to see the highlights?

Not at all — three nights covers a rich sequence of Nile temples and sites: Kom Ombo, Gebel el-Silsila, Edfu, and El Kab, ending in Luxor. It is ideal for travellers with limited time who still want the full dahabiya experience.
What is included on board?

Your cabin, all meals featuring local and international cuisine, the daily sunset afternoon tea on deck, and the crew are included. Temple entrance fees, guides, and transfers are arranged for you and quoted with your charter.
Can we sail from Luxor to Aswan instead?
Yes. The same highlights can be arranged in the reverse direction to suit your arrival city — just let us know and we will tailor it.

6n-7d-aswan-dendera-luxor
Aswan ⇄ Dendera ⇄ Luxor
Our signature "Long Cruise" — the classic southern route deepened with an exclusive excursion to Abydos and the magnificent Temple of Dendera.

A deep exploration of the Nile, combining the classic temples of the south with a rare pilgrimage to Abydos and Dendera — two of Egypt's most sacred and least-visited cult centres, often missed on standard itineraries. The schedule balances iconic sights with serene sailing.
Dendera & Abydos
aswan-dendera-luxor
Philae Temple
Edfu
Abydos
Dendera
Karnak
/images/Dendera_temple2.jpg

Six nights threading the Nile's great temples, with an exclusive full day ashore at Abydos and Dendera. Sailable in either direction — beginning amid the islands of Aswan, or with the grandeur of Thebes — both reaching the sublime reliefs of the Temple of Seti I and Dendera's painted astronomical ceiling.
Temples
9+ major sites
Relaxed · flexible
Aswan → Dendera → Luxor
Journey Through the Ages

This 6-night "Long Cruise" offers a deep exploration of the Nile, combining the classic southern route with an exclusive excursion to the magnificent Temple of Dendara. The schedule is designed to make the most of each day, balancing iconic sights with serene sailing.
Embarkation & The Goddess Isis's Sanctuary
Arrive in Aswan and step aboard — your journey begins on the island of Isis.
/images/philae_temple.jpg
Afternoon
Arrive in Aswan, board your dahabiya, and settle in.
Late afternoon
Visit the beautiful Philae Temple — a motorboat to Agilkia Island reveals this Graeco-Roman complex dedicated to Isis, famed for its elegant columns and picturesque setting.
Docked in Aswan.
The Dual Temple & Ancient Sandstone Quarries
Two great riverside monuments, with serene sailing in between.
/images/The_Dual_Temple_of_Sobek&Haroeris.jpg
Set sail north towards Kom Ombo.
Visit the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to two gods — the crocodile-headed Sobek and the falcon-headed Horus.
Continue sailing to the historic site of El Silsila.
Explore the El Silsila quarries — the source of sandstone for Egypt's great temples — with cliff-side chapels and royal stelae carved into the rock.
Sail onwards to Edfu.
Docked in Edfu.
The Falcon God's Citadel & Sailing to Thebes
Egypt's best-preserved temple, then a full day on the water.
/images/horus_temple.jpg
Visit the remarkably preserved Temple of Edfu, the largest dedicated to the god Horus — its massive pylon, courtyards, and intact inner halls a highlight of Ptolemaic architecture.
Depart Edfu for a full day of scenic sailing towards Luxor, ancient Thebes.
Docked in Luxor.
Theban Necropolis & Voyage to the Goddess Hathor
Valley of the Kings
Temple of Hatshepsut
Colossi of Memnon
The West Bank of Thebes, then a special sail north toward Dendara.
/images/Temple_of_Hatshepsut.jpg
Cross the Nile to the West Bank — the Valley of the Kings, the Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon.
A special northward sail to the city of Qena, the docking point for Dendara.
Docked near Dendara.
Pilgrimage to Abydos & The Temple of Hathor
Temple of Seti I
Dendera Temple
A full day ashore at two of Egypt's most sacred, least-visited temples.
/images/Temple_of_Seti.jpg
Full day · by road
Travel to Abydos — the magnificent Temple of Seti I, with its exquisite raised reliefs and the mystical Abydos King List, and the nearby Osireion.
Return to Qena for the stunning Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor — its famous astronomical ceiling, vivid column capitals, and rooftop chapels.
Evening
Set sail back to Luxor.
The Grand Temples of Thebes
Karnak Temple
Luxor Temple
The grand finale, on the East Bank of ancient Thebes.
Full day
Stand within the colossal Karnak Temple Complex — the forest of pillars in the Great Hypostyle Hall — then the elegant Luxor Temple on the Nile Corniche.
Disembarkation
Farewell to the river.
/images/more_than_plan.jpg
After breakfast, disembark in Luxor, concluding your deep journey through Egyptian history and spirituality.
Luxor → Dendera → Aswan
Sacred Journey South

This six-night itinerary offers a strategic and enriching route. First explore the wonders of ancient Thebes (Luxor), then embark on a special excursion north to the cult temples of Abydos and Dendara, before a relaxing downriver sail to Aswan — concluding with the beautiful sites of the south.

Strategic route — this direction lets you experience the grandeur of Luxor first, when energy is highest, followed by the special excursion. The upriver sail from Luxor to Aswan can allow for a more relaxed pace.
Abydos & Dendera — the Day 3 excursion is a significant full-day road trip from Qena, requiring an early start, but is uniquely rewarding for those drawn to deep historical and religious sites.
Aswan as a finale — ending in Aswan offers a natural conclusion, with the option to extend your stay toward the temples of Abu Simbel, a highlight of any southern Egypt itinerary.
Embarkation in Thebes & The East Bank
Board in Luxor and begin amid the grandeur of ancient Thebes.
Arrive and board your dahabiya in Luxor.

Explore the East Bank — the colossal Karnak Temple Complex, a city of temples, obelisks, and the forest of 134 columns in the Great Hypostyle Hall — then the graceful Luxor Temple, illuminated at dusk.
The Theban West Bank
The necropolis of ancient Thebes, before the special excursion north.
A full day ashore at two of Egypt's most significant temples.
Travel to Abydos — the magnificent Temple of Seti I, with its exquisite raised reliefs and the mystical Abydos King List.
Return to the Qena area for the stunning Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor — its vividly painted astronomical ceiling, crypts, and iconic Hathor-headed columns.
Sail back south from Qena to Luxor.
Sailing South via Esna to Edfu
A full day of relaxed sailing after the intensive temple days.
Day
Sail downriver towards Edfu, passing through the Esna Lock — a feat of modern engineering that regulates the river's flow — as rural Egyptian life unfolds along the banks.
Docked near Edfu.
Edfu, El Silsila & Kom Ombo
Three monuments and a final serene sail to Aswan.
Visit the monumental Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus.
Sail south from Edfu to El Silsila.
Explore the sandstone quarries of El Silsila — the cliff-side Speos of Horemheb and royal stelae carved into the rock faces.
Sail onwards to Kom Ombo.
Visit the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to both Sobek and Horus — its riverside setting beautiful in the late-afternoon light.
A final serene sail to Aswan.
The Island of Isis
High Dam
Old Dam
Unfinished Obelisk
Aswan's sacred island and its modern marvels.
Visit the beautiful Philae Temple — a short motorboat ride to Agilkia Island reveals this Graeco-Roman complex dedicated to Isis, famed for its elegant pillars.
On the way back, see Aswan's High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
Disembarkation in Aswan
Farewell to the river — with Abu Simbel within reach.
After breakfast, disembark in Aswan — with the option to extend your stay toward the temples of Abu Simbel.
What makes this the "Long Cruise"?

The inclusion of Abydos makes this uniquely comprehensive, offering access to one of Egypt's most sacred and historically significant sites — the Temple of Seti I — alongside Dendera, both often missed on standard itineraries.
How demanding is the Abydos & Dendera day?

It is a full-day road excursion from Qena (roughly 1.5 hours each way to Abydos) requiring an early start, but it is uniquely rewarding for travellers drawn to deep historical and spiritual sites. Comfortable shoes, sun protection, and water are essential.
Which direction will we sail?

Either Aswan to Luxor or Luxor to Aswan — the temples are the same, the order differs. Starting in Luxor lets you experience the grandeur of Thebes first; ending in Aswan opens the option to extend toward Abu Simbel. We tailor the direction to your travel plans.
Can the order of visits change?

Yes. This is a private, flexible charter and a general framework — your guide may adjust the sequence of visits, especially on the West Bank, to ensure the best experience based on river, lock, and site conditions.

Day 5 — Abydos & Dendera is a full-day road excursion (about 1.5 hours each way from Qena to Abydos), requiring an early start. The day-by-day reflects a realistic timeline for this exceptional day of sightseeing.
The inclusion of Abydos makes this a uniquely comprehensive "Long Cruise," offering access to one of Egypt's most sacred and historically significant sites — often missed on standard itineraries.
The sequence of visits, especially on the West Bank, may be arranged by your guide to ensure the best possible experience based on conditions.

13n-14d-cairo-aswan
Cairo ⇄ Aswan
The entire length of the Nile — thirteen nights linking the Pyramids of Giza with the temples of Aswan, the most complete dahabiya voyage we offer.

Discover the whole river aboard a traditional dahabiya, towed for the long northern reaches and sailing the classic south. From Giza and the Fayoum through Middle Egypt's hidden tombs to Abydos, Dendera, Thebes, Edfu, Kom Ombo, and Aswan — daily international buffet dining and the signature sunset tea on deck throughout.
Grand Journey
cairo-aswan
Giza Pyramids
Tell el-Amarna
/images/nubian-village.jpg

Thirteen nights from the Mediterranean delta to the Nubian south — Giza, Fayoum, Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, Abydos and Dendera, the monuments of Luxor, then Edfu, Kom Ombo, and the island temple of Philae at Aswan. Sailable Cairo → Aswan (southbound, against the current) or Aswan → Cairo (northbound, with the current).

Is this the most complete Nile itinerary?

Yes — thirteen nights covers the entire navigable Nile between Cairo and Aswan, combining Middle Egypt's rarely-seen monuments with the classic temples of the south, all aboard a single traditional dahabiya.
How does the towing work, and why?

For the long northern reaches a support tugboat tows the dahabiya, allowing an authentic, leisurely cruise over distances no sailing dahabiya could cover alone. Southbound (Cairo → Aswan) runs against the current and is around 15–20% slower than the northbound direction.
Which direction should we choose?

Both visit the same sites. Cairo → Aswan builds gradually toward the southern temples; Aswan → Cairo flies you south to begin and sails home with the current. We tailor the direction to your flights and preferences.
This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long northern reaches — sailing times are far longer than conventional cruisers, for an authentic, leisurely passage.
Thirteen nights covers the entire navigable Nile, combining Middle Egypt's rarely-seen monuments with the classic temples of the south aboard a single vessel.

5n-6d-aswan-luxor
Aswan ⇄ Luxor
A classic five-night Nile voyage blending awe-inspiring temples with the tranquil rhythm of river life — sailable in either direction.

Journey through the heart of ancient Egypt between the engineering marvels of Aswan and the monumental wonders of Luxor. Five nights allow you to explore the major highlights at a relaxed pace, with the option of a breathtaking side trip to Abu Simbel.

The classic stretch of the Nile, sailed at a relaxed pace. Both directions visit the same great temples — Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, El Kab, and the monuments of Thebes — with the order tailored to your arrival city and an optional excursion to Abu Simbel.
Abu Simbel
Optional excursion
Classic · relaxed
Upstream Through the Ages

Journey through the heart of ancient Egypt on a classic Nile voyage, blending awe-inspiring temple visits with the tranquil rhythm of river life — from the engineering marvels of Aswan to the monumental wonders of Luxor.
Aswan — Embarkation & City Highlights
Board in Aswan and explore the city's ancient and modern marvels.
Arrival
Embark your dahabiya in Aswan and enjoy lunch on board.
Visit Philae Temple on Agilkia Island, the monumental High Dam that created Lake Nasser, the British-built Old Dam of 1902, and the Unfinished Obelisk in the granite quarries.
Dinner and your first night moored in Aswan.
Abu Simbel Option & Sailing to Kom Ombo
Abu Simbel (optional)

Morning · option B
A relaxed visit to Aswan's Botanical Gardens on Kitchener's Island and a traditional Nubian village.
Begin your cruise northward.
Disembark to explore the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to Sobek and Horus.
Moored near Edfu.
Edfu Temple & Onward to El Kab
Egypt's best-preserved temple, reached by horse-drawn carriage.
Travel by horse-drawn carriage to the immensely well-preserved Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus.
Midday
Cruise toward the ancient site of El Kab.
Enjoy the passing river scenery from the sundeck.
Moored near El Kab.
El Kab & Sailing to Luxor via Esna
An ancient city, then through the Esna lock to Luxor.
Explore the archaeological site of El Kab — an ancient city of tombs, temples, and rock inscriptions.
Cruise toward Luxor, passing through the Esna Lock (observed from deck) and the town of Esna.
Arrive in Luxor.
Luxor — Thebes, City of the Gods
Both banks of ancient Thebes in a single, unforgettable day.
Morning · West Bank
The Theban Necropolis — the Valley of the Kings, the Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the Colossi of Memnon.
Afternoon · East Bank
The magnificent Karnak Temple complex and the elegant Luxor Temple, beautifully lit at dusk.
Farewell dinner and final night on board in Luxor.
After breakfast on board, disembark at your leisure in Luxor, marking the end of your Nile cruise.
Luxor → Aswan
Downstream to Nubia

Sail south along the legendary Nile from the world's greatest open-air museum in Luxor to the graceful city of Aswan, exploring the major highlights at a relaxed pace — concluding with the option of a breathtaking side trip to Abu Simbel.
Luxor — Arrival & East Bank Wonders
Board in Luxor amid the grandeur of ancient Thebes.
Board your dahabiya in Luxor and enjoy a welcome lunch.
Explore the East Bank — the vast Karnak Temple complex of pylons and obelisks, and the majestic Luxor Temple, illuminated after dusk.
Dinner and your first night moored in Luxor.
Luxor's West Bank & Sailing South
The Theban necropolis, then south through the Esna lock.
Cross to the West Bank necropolis — the Valley of the Kings, the terraced Temple of Hatshepsut, and the Colossi of Memnon.
Begin your cruise south, passing through the Esna Lock.
Moored near the ancient site of El Kab.
El Kab & The Temple of Horus at Edfu
A pre-dynastic city and Egypt's best-preserved temple.
Explore the ruins of El Kab, an important pre-dynastic and Pharaonic settlement.
Cruise to Edfu.
Travel by horse-drawn carriage to the magnificent Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus.
Continue to the mooring at Gebel el-Silsila.
Moored near El Silsila.
Gebel el-Silsila & Kom Ombo
Ancient quarries and the double temple, then on to Aswan.
Discover Gebel el-Silsila — the ancient sandstone quarry with rock-cut shrines and royal stelae along the banks.
Cruise to Kom Ombo.
Tour the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to both Sobek and Horus.
Enjoy the final stretch of sailing toward Aswan.
Moored in Aswan.
Aswan's Treasures & Abu Simbel Option
A full day with two ways to experience the south.
Option 1 · Aswan
Visit the sublime island Temple of Philae, the High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
Option 2 · Abu Simbel
A very early excursion (by plane or convoy) to the colossal rock-cut temples of Abu Simbel, returning to Aswan's highlights in the afternoon.
Farewell dinner and final night on board in Aswan.
After breakfast on board, disembark at your leisure in Aswan, marking the end of your cruise.

Both directions visit the same temples; only the order and the sailing flow differ. Sailing from Luxor to Aswan is upriver and can feel more relaxed, and ends near Abu Simbel for an optional extension. We tailor the direction to your arrival city.
Is Abu Simbel included?
Abu Simbel is offered as an optional excursion by plane or convoy on the Aswan day. We arrange it on request and quote it with your charter.
Are entrance fees and guides included?
The boat, all meals on board, and the crew are included. Temple entrance fees, expert guides, and land transfers are arranged for you and quoted with your private charter.

12n-13d-cairo-luxor
Cairo ⇄ Luxor
The full sweep of the Nile aboard a traditional dahabiya under tow — from the Pyramids of Giza through Middle Egypt's hidden monuments to the temple cities of Thebes.

A rare twelve-night voyage along the river's least-travelled reaches, linking Cairo with Luxor. Traditional dahabiya cruising, gently towed by a support tugboat for the long passage, with daily international buffet dining and the signature sunset tea on deck.
cairo-luxor
Fayoum Pyramids

Twelve nights spanning Middle and Upper Egypt — the pyramids of Giza and Fayoum, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera, and the grand monuments of Luxor. Sailable in either direction; northbound (Luxor → Cairo) runs with the current and is noticeably faster.
Cairo → Luxor
Upstream Through Egypt

Experience the timeless beauty of the Nile aboard a traditional dahabiya, elegantly towed by a support tugboat for long-distance navigation. Sailing southbound against the current, the days are unhurried — the point of this slow, authentic river journey.

Luxor → Cairo

Sailing northbound from Luxor to Cairo with the Nile current, this direction runs roughly 15–20% faster than the southbound route — beginning amid the grandeur of Thebes and ending at the Pyramids of Giza.

Old Cairo & Disembarkation Preparations
Old Cairo

Optional
Old Cairo and the Khan el-Khalili bazaar, or leisure on board.
Why are the sailing times so long?

This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long Cairo–Luxor passage. Sailing times are far longer than conventional Nile cruisers — by design. The reward is the slow, authentic river and access to Middle Egypt's rarely-visited monuments.
Which direction is faster?
Northbound (Luxor → Cairo) sails with the Nile current and is roughly 15–20% faster than the southbound Cairo → Luxor route. Both visit the same sites; we tailor the direction to your travel plans.
What makes this itinerary special?

Very few vessels sail the full Cairo–Luxor reach. You see Giza and the Fayoum pyramids, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, and the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera — alongside the great monuments of Thebes.
This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long Cairo passage — sailing times are far longer than conventional Nile cruisers, by design, for a slow and authentic journey.
The route reaches Middle Egypt's rarely-visited monuments — Fayoum, Tell el-Amarna, and Beni Hassan — alongside the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera and the great monuments of Thebes.

7n-8d-aswan-luxor
A leisurely seven-night sail along the classic Nile route, tracing the path of the pharaohs from Aswan to the monumental city of Thebes, with time to linger at every temple and island.

Embark on a timeless voyage down the Nile, tracing the path of the pharaohs from Aswan to the monumental city of Thebes (Luxor). This seven-night classic cruise blends profound historical discovery with serene river travel — from the island temple of Philae and the engineering marvels of Aswan through Kom Ombo, El Silsila, and Edfu to the Theban Necropolis, Karnak, and Luxor Temple.
Aswan High Dam

Seven unhurried nights on the classic stretch of the Nile, with extra time ashore and on deck — Philae and Aswan's monuments, Kom Ombo, El Silsila, Edfu, and the great temples and tombs of Thebes. Sailable in either direction.
Extended · slow
Journey Through Ancient Egypt

Embark on a timeless voyage down the Nile, tracing the path of the pharaohs from Aswan to the monumental city of Thebes. This seven-night classic cruise blends profound historical discovery with serene river travel.
Embarkation & The Island of Isis

Board your cruise ship in Aswan and begin your exploration with a visit to the exquisite Philae Temple. Accessed by a short motorboat ride, this temple complex dedicated to the goddess Isis was rescued from the rising Nile and rebuilt on Agilkia Island, showcasing stunning Ptolemaic architecture.

Board your cruise ship in Aswan and visit the exquisite Philae Temple on Agilkia Island — dedicated to the goddess Isis and rebuilt after being rescued from the rising Nile, showcasing stunning Ptolemaic architecture.
Overnight docked in Aswan.
Modern Engineering & Ancient Ambitions
Discover Aswan's modern history and ancient industry before setting sail north along the scenic Nile.

Visit the Aswan High Dam, a pivotal 20th-century feat of engineering controlling the Nile's floodwaters. See the Old Aswan Dam and the fascinating Unfinished Obelisk, which offers a unique glimpse into ancient stone-cutting techniques.
Set sail north, enjoying the scenic river views.
Overnight moored at the tranquil El Herdiab Island.
The Dual Temple & Sacred Quarries
El Silsila
Sail to Kom Ombo for its unique double temple, then continue to the historic quarry site of El Silsila.
Sail to Kom Ombo.

Visit the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, symmetrically dedicated to two gods: the falcon-headed Horus the Elder and the crocodile-headed Sobek. Explore its halls, see ancient mummified crocodiles, and enjoy its dramatic riverside location.
Overnight docked at El Silsila.
Sandstone Source & The Falcon's Citadel
Explore the great sandstone quarry of ancient Egypt, then sail to Edfu to visit the largest and best-preserved temple dedicated to Horus.
Explore El Silsila, the major sandstone quarry of ancient Egypt. Tour the cliff-side shrines and see the famous "Speos of Horemheb" cut into the rock.
Sail to Edfu.

Visit the incredibly well-preserved Temple of Edfu, the largest temple dedicated to the god Horus. Its colossal pylons, courtyard, and intact inner chambers make it one of Egypt's most complete ancient monuments.
Overnight docked near Edfu (at El Fawaza Island).
Sailing North & The Esna Lock
A relaxed day on the sun deck as your ship sails toward Luxor, passing through the Esna Lock.
Relax on the sun deck as your ship sails towards Luxor. Witness the fascinating process of passing through the Esna Lock, a modern gateway that regulates the river's flow.
Arrive in Luxor, the ancient city of Thebes, for an overnight stay.
Thebes' Necropolis — City of the Dead
A full day exploring the vast Theban Necropolis on the West Bank of Luxor.

Cross to the West Bank of Luxor to explore the vast Theban Necropolis. Highlights typically include the Valley of the Kings (entrance to three tombs included, often excluding Tutankhamun's), the imposing Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon.
Overnight docked in Luxor.
Thebes' Grandest Monuments — Karnak & Luxor
The colossal temples of the East Bank — Karnak and Luxor — the largest religious complex of the ancient world.
Visit the colossal Karnak Temple Complex, a vast open-air museum and the largest religious site in the ancient world. Walk through the awe-inspiring Great Hypostyle Hall with its 134 massive columns.
Afternoon / Evening
Explore the elegant Luxor Temple, beautifully located on the Corniche. Its grandeur is especially captivating at dusk when the stones are illuminated.
Final overnight on board in Luxor.
After breakfast, disembark in Luxor, concluding your journey through millennia of history.
After breakfast, disembark from your cruise ship in Luxor, concluding your journey through millennia of history.

Sail south from the monumental city of Thebes to the graceful islands of Aswan, exploring the great temples and tombs of Luxor before the classic river route through Edfu, El Silsila, and Kom Ombo to Philae.
Embarkation & The Temples of Thebes
Board your dahabiya in Luxor amid the grandeur of ancient Thebes, exploring the great temples of the East Bank.
Board your cruise ship in Luxor. Visit the colossal Karnak Temple Complex and its Great Hypostyle Hall of 134 columns, then the elegant Luxor Temple on the Corniche, especially captivating at dusk.
Cross to the West Bank of Luxor to explore the Theban Necropolis — the Valley of the Kings, the imposing Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon.
Sailing South & The Esna Lock
A relaxed day on the sun deck as your ship sails south toward Edfu, passing through the Esna Lock.
Relax on the sun deck as your ship sails south, passing through the Esna Lock, the modern gateway that regulates the river's flow.
Overnight docked near Edfu.
The Falcon's Citadel at Edfu
Visit the largest and best-preserved temple dedicated to Horus, then continue sailing toward El Silsila.
Visit the incredibly well-preserved Temple of Edfu, the largest temple dedicated to the god Horus, with its colossal pylons, courtyard, and intact inner chambers.
Sail south toward El Silsila.
Sandstone Quarries & Kom Ombo
The great sandstone quarry of ancient Egypt in the morning, then the unique double temple of Kom Ombo.
Explore El Silsila, the major sandstone quarry of ancient Egypt, with its cliff-side shrines and the famous "Speos of Horemheb".
Sail to Kom Ombo and visit its unique double temple, dedicated to Horus the Elder and Sobek, with its ancient mummified crocodiles.
Overnight docked near Kom Ombo.
Modern Engineering at Aswan
Continue sailing to Aswan to discover its modern history and ancient industry.
Continue sailing south to Aswan, enjoying the scenic river views.
Visit the Aswan High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk, which offers a unique glimpse into ancient stone-cutting techniques.
A visit to the exquisite island temple of Philae, dedicated to the goddess Isis.
Visit the exquisite Philae Temple on Agilkia Island — dedicated to the goddess Isis and rebuilt after being rescued from the rising Nile, showcasing stunning Ptolemaic architecture.
Free time to enjoy the calm of Aswan.
Final overnight on board in Aswan.
After breakfast, disembark in Aswan, concluding your journey through millennia of history.
After breakfast, disembark from your cruise ship in Aswan, concluding your Nile journey.

This itinerary provides a general framework; the order of visits on specific days may be adjusted by your tour operator. Comfortable walking shoes, sun protection, and water are essential for daily excursions.

4n-5d-aswan-luxor
A four-night classic between Aswan and Luxor — the Nile's essential temples at an easy, unhurried pace.
Four nights sailing between Aswan and Luxor, taking in Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, and the great monuments of Thebes. Sailable in either direction.

Sail north with the current from the island temples of Aswan to the monumental wonders of Luxor — Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, Gebel el-Silsila, and the great temples of Thebes, all in four unhurried nights aboard your dahabiya.
Aswan — Arrival & City Highlights

Embark your dahabiya in Aswan and explore the city's ancient and modern marvels — the beautiful Philae Temple on Agilkia Island, the monumental High Dam, the British-built Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
Embark on your dahabeya in Aswan and enjoy lunch on board.
Visit the beautiful Philae Temple on Agilkia Island, followed by the High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
Kom Ombo & Sailing to Silsila
Tour the unique double Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to both Sobek and Haroeris, then sail north to El Silsila to explore its ancient sandstone quarries and riverside shrines.
Tour the unique Kom Ombo Temple, dedicated to both Sobek and Haroeris.
Sail to El Silsila to explore its ancient sandstone quarries and riverside shrines.
Overnight moored near El Silsila.
Edfu & Sailing to Esna
Visit the remarkably preserved Temple of Horus at Edfu, then continue sailing north through the Esna Lock toward Luxor.
Visit the remarkably preserved Temple of Horus in Edfu.
Continue sailing north, passing through the Esna Lock toward Luxor.
Overnight docked near Esna.
Luxor — Temples of Thebes

Discover the legendary West Bank of Luxor — the royal tombs, the Temple of Hatshepsut, and the Colossi of Memnon — then cross to the East Bank for the vast Karnak Temple complex and the elegant Luxor Temple along the Nile.
Discover the legendary West Bank of Luxor, home to royal tombs, the Temple of Hatshepsut, and the Colossi of Memnon.
Explore the vast Karnak Temple complex, then visit the elegant Luxor Temple along the Nile.
Luxor — Departure
After breakfast on board, disembark in Luxor, marking the end of your Nile voyage.
Disembark after breakfast by 8:00 AM.

Sail south from the world's greatest open-air museum in Luxor to the graceful city of Aswan — the Temples of Thebes, the West Bank necropolis, Edfu, Gebel el-Silsila, Kom Ombo, and the island temple of Philae, all in four unhurried nights aboard your dahabiya.
Luxor — Arrival & Temples of Thebes

Embark your dahabiya in Luxor and step straight into the grandeur of ancient Thebes. In the late afternoon, explore the vast Karnak Temple complex before visiting the elegant Luxor Temple along the Nile.
Embark on your dahabeya in Luxor.
West Bank & Sailing to Edfu
Discover the legendary West Bank of Luxor — the royal tombs, the terraced Temple of Hatshepsut, and the Colossi of Memnon. In the afternoon, sail south through the Esna Lock and continue toward Edfu.
Sail south, passing through the Esna Lock, and continue toward Edfu.
Overnight docked in Edfu.
Edfu, Silsila & Kom Ombo
Visit the remarkably preserved Temple of Horus at Edfu, then sail to El Silsila to explore its ancient sandstone quarries and riverside shrines, continuing on to Kom Ombo.
Sail to El Silsila to explore its ancient sandstone quarries and riverside shrines. Continue sailing to Kom Ombo.
Overnight moored in Kom Ombo.
Kom Ombo & Aswan Highlights

Tour the unique double Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to both Sobek and Haroeris. Sail on to Aswan, where you visit the beautiful Philae Temple on Agilkia Island, followed by the High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
Sail to Aswan. Upon arrival, visit the beautiful Philae Temple on Agilkia Island, followed by the High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk.
Aswan — Departure
After breakfast on board, disembark in Aswan, marking the end of your Nile voyage.

veda

The Nile rewards a slower pace. Over the seasons we've collected stories worth telling — about the kitchen, the crew, the temples, and the small decisions that shape a charter.

This is where they'll live.

We'll publish a few times a season — no calendar, no churn. Letters from the boats when something is worth saying.

— The VEDA team
